name: developer
description: Unified development experience for building, evolving, and shipping agent-index collections — from first idea through marketplace submission. v1.7.0: ship-side codification — new `release` task generates a host-native, preflight-gated, version-gated release push script (code-repos-first/listings-last ordering, per-repo commit→push→tag v<version>, never-move-a-published-tag, backend-distribution publish handoff) — the ship-side counterpart to core's clone-script-generator, turning the hand-copied push-*.sh tribal knowledge into a generated, repeatable step; develop's lifecycle Release stage now names tagging + /shared/dist/ publish and routes to @ai:release; a standalone release-checklist reference resolves the lifecycle's dangling reference; preflight adds a release-readiness note. v1.5.0: access-model currency — develop teaches the three access patterns (open-commons / owned-content / two-tier hybrid) with the verified-outcome grant gate, pointer conventions, org sharing vocabulary, collaborative-acls.json scaffolding and id:-anchor guidance; preflight gains Step 7.5 Access-Model Consistency (errors on shared writes without ACL provisioning and on retired constructs); optimize Step 7 example modernized to the pointer-index model. Earlier: see CHANGELOG.
author: agent-index

triggers.description: Start or continue developing a collection
Open the development workflow for a collection
Continue building a collection in progress
Run pre-submission checks on a collection
Run final checks before marketplace submission
Get a guided intro to collection development
Analyze and improve a collection's performance
Run optimization analysis on a collection
Generate the host-native push+tag script for a release
Produce the preflight-gated release push script
